title: WHO IS A GREAT SOUL? | Bhagavad Gita Verses 9.14 - 9.15 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, today we will be discussing the 14th and 15th verse from the 9th chapter of the Gita.
So, here Krishna describes the characteristic of a Mahatma or a great soul. Who is a great soul?
So, before we get into this discussion, Srila Prabhupada in the purpose of this particular verses explains some very, very important and crucial points.
More so in present times.
In present times, we see a lot of people claiming to be God and we see plethora of God-men surround.
And we also see a lot of people following different God-men, worshipping them, surrendering to them, sometimes bordering towards blind faith.
And at times we see even scandal-slamming.
And people feeling cheated and left broken-hearted.
So, Srila Prabhupada was once asked, have you seen God?
As I mentioned in one of our earlier discussion, Srila Prabhupada on different occasions, because this was a very, very commonly asked question, frequently asked question.
Srila Prabhupada gave different answers.
Srila Prabhupada gave different answers.
Srila Prabhupada gave different answers.
But one answer which he used to give is that actually it's not a very intelligent question.
Because if I say, yes, I have seen God, in one sense you have no way to verify it.
I can always say, yes, I have seen God, I am God.
And there's no way of verifying it.
Srila Prabhupada corrected that person and said,
And a more intelligent question would be, can anyone help me?
What is a more relevant and intelligent question if you want to ask the same very question?
How can I see God?
Okay, anybody else?
How can we?
How can we reach God?
Anybody else?
How can we reach God?
So he said, rather more intelligent question would be, what are the symptoms of a person who has seen God?
Isn't it?
It makes sense.
Rather than asking, is this gold?
And if I am very dead sure that you don't know anything about gold, I can say, yes, this is gold.
This is 24 karat gold.
Pay me 50 lakh rupees for this.
And imagine you don't know anything.
And somehow you trust me.
There is some element of faith.
Who knows, you are wealthy, you may even pay.
I can say, yes, this is gold.
But the probability of getting cheated is much lesser or blindly believing anything.
If you were to ask me a question, what are the characteristics of pure gold?
How does it look?
How can we find out that a particular object is gold?
So in this particular verse,
Prabhupada explains, or rather Krishna explains, that's how Gita is foolproof in case we have read Gita very thoroughly and understood it.
Then we will see that in this world, unfortunately, many people are getting royally cheated in the name of spirituality.
People are being given fake gold in the name of gold and people are willing to pay the price.
Imagine,
what will happen if you see actually a person buying this for 50 lakh rupees in the name of gold.
Will you not feel bad?
So that happens when you are blessed with the right understanding and you see people around, unfortunately, getting cheated.
So based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada, today we will cover these two particular verses, very important verses.
And let's chant this verse together.
Satatam keerthayandam.
Nitya yuktavapasyate.
Namasyantasya maam bhaktiya.
Nitya yuktavapasyate.
Before we go into the translation, what does satatam mean?
Satatam means always.
Always.
So, the quality of a great soul or a Mahatma is Satatam. Satatam what? Kirtanto. Whom?
Maam. So, one of the symptoms of a great soul or a Mahatma or a genuine God-man is always
chanting the glories of the Lord. Not at the time of a program, always. 24 bar 7. Satatam
kirtayanto maam. Kirtan doesn't mean only, you know the kirtan typical we have that understanding.
Kirtan will come to that. Kirtan means glorifying the Lord. Glorifying the pastimes of the Lord.
Glorifying the holy name of the Lord. Glorifying the pastimes of the Lord. Glorifying the holy
name of the Lord. Glorifying the activities of the Lord. Will come to that. Always chanting
my glories. Endeavouring with great determination. Endeavouring for what? To always remember
Him. To always be conscious of Him. In our present state, we are not that conscious.
We are conscious of so many things. Our attention is splashed on so many objects, so many people,
so many things. The determination is trying how to put back that attention which has got
splashed on so many things back to the Lord. Endeavouring with great determination, it's
going against the current. It's not an easy thing. To forget God is relatively easy. To
remember God in present times, it's relatively tough. So, endeavouring with great determination,
it is tough, it's a struggle. It's not an easy thing but endeavouring with great determination.
Bowing down before me. Literally in the sense, if you pay obeisance is bowing down means
surrendering. Surrendering to the instructions of the Lord. Following what the Lord has instructed
us to do. Obeying the instructions of the Lord and the great Acharyas. These great souls
perpetually worship me.
with devotion. What is the meaning of perpetuity? Always, perpetually. Perpetuity is for all
times to come. Worship me with devotion. Now the symptoms of Mahatma are being described.
So if we understand what are the symptoms of a genuine Mahatma, genuine God-man, a genuine
self-realized soul, then there is relatively less possibility of we getting cheated. He
is always engaged in the chanting of the glories of the Lord. What is the glorification of
the Lord? Kirtayanta, we talked about. It's chanting His holy names, remembering His form,
eternal form, His transcendental qualities.
If you see all the prayers of great Acharyas, great sages in the Bhagavatam and any other
literatures, they are always glorifying the Lord. His activities, His qualities, His wonderful
leelas. So these great sages are always absorbed in that transcendental qualities, His uncommon
pastimes.
So, what are the symptoms of Mahatma? If you see all the prayers of great Acharyas, great
sages in the Bhagavatam, genuine God-man, a genuine self-realized soul, genuine God-man,
genuine self-realization, genuine God-man, He is always engaging in the worship of the
Lord.
And they are very very attached to the Supreme Lord. Last class, we talked about two kinds
of Bhakti. Anyone remembers what are the two types? Sadhana Bhakti and Raganuga Bhakti.
Ninth chapter is Krishna talks about Bhakti Yoga. Sadhana Bhakti is, when we do Bhakti
or do some devotional activities.
devotional activities without having attachment to the Lord. How many of us have genuine attachment
to the Lord? Genuine. The right answer would be, we are trying to revive that attachment.
Right now, our attachment is, as I mentioned, to so many things. Lord is one of the many
things I am attached to. So, in the Sadhana Bhakti stage, our attachment is to so many
things, so many people. But still we are doing some devotional activities. It's mainly not
driven by attachment to the Lord.
It is driven by
obedience to superior authorities. I may not relish those activities
in the present state. For example, getting up early in the morning. Attending the Mangal
art. It may not be very relishable. I may feel like to lie back in the bed and be there
in the bed for
additional one hour is more relishable, relatively. On a fasting day, to fast on Ekadarshi and
not eat anything is not relishable, it's not very pleasing. So initially when we start
off, there are many rules and regulations which may not be very palatable, very pleasing
to us, but we do it because we have been asked by higher authorities to do it. Just like
the doctor, you may give a medicine which is bitter, may not be very tasty, we may not
like taking it. I mentioned when I was diagnosed with some deficiency on B12, the doctor said
you should take 7 days injection. So I didn't like going to the hospital and taking an injection
every day, but the doctor said it's important and I did that. Injection is not going all
the way to the hospital, spending time, spending money and then taking the medicine. It's
taking a jab, it's painful. Doctor said do it and I have done it. It is for my good.
So we have examples how many times we do when somebody who is having a higher understanding
on a particular subject, asks us to do a particular thing, we have faith and we do it. So likewise
initially when we do devotional activities, it is driven not by attraction to the Lord,
but it is driven by obedience to higher authorities.
And that bhakti is called Sādhanabhakti. And when we do Sādhanabhakti, because of
the obedience to the higher authorities, we get their blessings. In fact, not because
of our efforts, because of their blessings, their mercy, because we have endeavored to
follow them. Follow their instructions in spite of some
inconvenience.
is, by their blessings, what do we get? We get Raga. What is Raga? Attraction and attachment
to the Lord. And that Bhakti which starts with Sadhana Bhakti, graduates to Raga Nuga
Bhakti. Then there is no force. Then it is done with taste. One relishes that. Then we
are not in a mood that when will my chanting get completed. Then we want to do more and
more. So, right now all of us are in a state of Sadhana Bhakti. It's all about rules and
regulations. It is about the rules and regulations. It is about the rules and regulations. It

Tapasya. Tapasya means voluntarily accepting some inconveniences for a higher cause. Getting up early in the morning, not eating particular things. It may be very relishable for the tongue. But it's said don't eat it.
So, when we do Sadhana Bhakti, we are blessed because we are following the superior authorities, Acharyas, just like we are following a doctor.
When the doctor told me to take injections for seven days, I was blessed in one sense. What way? The deficiency that I had and the symptoms I had, I recovered from that. I regained my health.
So, determined to…now, another quality of Sadhana Bhakti…
Sadhana Bhakti.
Mahatma is determined to achieve the ultimate end, the association of Supreme Lord, always in association.
Right now, in one sense, we are de-linked. Our relationship although is eternal, but we have forgotten that relationship.
That's why Yoga means…what is the meaning of Yoga? To link, to connect, to reunite. Bhakti Yoga. Karma Yoga. Jnana Yoga.
So, that relationship which is forgotten, which is now severed, who has severed that relationship? Because of whom that relationship has got forgotten? Because of?
Because of Maya. We should blame Maya. Whom we should blame? Our senses. Anybody else whom to blame?
Our ego.
Our ego.
Our ego.
What else?
Myself. Yes. My wrong exercise of free will.
Made wrong choices and I have forgotten the Lord.
Janiya suniyya vishya khainu.
Knowingly I have…knowingly, unknowingly I have drunk poison.
One of the Acharyas prays, knowingly, unknowingly I have drunk poison.
So we will come to that, what poison he is referring to.
He engages all activities, mental, bodily and vocal, everything in the service of the
Lord, Shri Krishna, Maam.
Maam means, who is telling this, Lord Krishna is telling.
Tan, man, dhan, with his body, with his mind, with his wealth, everything, whatever I have,
whatever strength I have, whatever abilities I have, whatever talents I have, whatever
skills I have, it is all yours.
So Mahatma is cent percent, he doesn't have any other part-time business.
He is completely cent percent engaged in the service of the Lord.
His body, his mind, his senses, his abilities, his talent, understanding that, I am the Lord.
Mahatma's great souls strictly observe all the rules and regulations.
Although they may be great souls, but still, sometimes we have seen examples of so-called
God-men, themselves not following some of the rules.
And when they were asked, they said that no, this is required in the beginning stages.
And once you cross a particular threshold level, then it's all not mandatory.
Could be dabbling with women.
Could be following some particular rules.
You can relax on all those things.
Initially it is required as stepping stone.
Once you reach that level of transcendence, then you can all be liberal.
Whereas Mahatmas, although they may be transcendentally situated.
example to lead people on the right path they walk the talk in other words their
character is pristine and it is not just a show many times people put up a show
just like in film we see actors they put up a show they act like a old man they
act like a rich man they act like a this thing so you can also put up a show and a
gullible man if you know the show is good if a person acts well if he's dressed well
it matches with the quality of sadhu it's easy to to for people to believe for example fasting
one
person strictly follows all the rules now I have a very fundamental question we were
talking about soul great soul Mahatma Bhagavad Gita Krishna many many places talks about soul
in fact the second chapter starts with the understanding of soul in reply to
Arjuna's question dilemma confusion Krishna introduces the concept of a soul
what is a soul and how many of us have seen a soul seen the soul felt the soul
experienced the soul seen the soul
okay so let me share you on a lighter note Shila Prabhupada was once asked
Prabhupada when I start chanting when I chant very feelingly I can see some you
know green light white light and that you know sparkling some kind of stars
and
what is this experience is there some kind of a spiritual experience
so Shila Prabhupada being a great acharya he said
you continue chanting in the beginning stages you will see all the stars slowly as you continue
it will all go away
, so in other words let's
what is a soul. We have been talking about it, all the sessions have some understanding
of soul. This reminds me, I briefly covered today morning also, when I was in the final
year in my college, the 8th semester, I was I think maybe 21 years that time, I had a
very close friend of mine, we were staying together, we studied together, same batch,
staying in the same hostel, his room was in the vicinity, close to my room and we used
to spend lot of time together. Not one of my closest friend but yes, very close. So
one night, generally after having dinner, we used to go for a walk and then we had dinner
together.
And I asked him, let's go for a walk. He said, no, today I am not feeling well, I am
feeling feverish, let me take rest, I am feeling very tired, feverish. So I said okay. So his
room was on the way, so I left him in the room, I said, need any help, please call me
and then he lied down on the bed and then I continued, I went for a walk, we had some
other friends also. And when we came back, one of the friends told me that he has, looks
like, his health is not good, he is having lot of fever. So then we quickly arranged for
a thermometer, went and checked and by that time he had, you know started some kind of
shivering, so we could make out that no, temperature is there, maybe fear has risen and then as
usual when somebody become sick, so people start, you know, okay, let's get this, let's
get that, let's try to do this, should we call a doctor and then we said, no, it's normal
fever.
So one of them suggested.
So let's give him some medicine.
some crocin, paracetamol and it will subside. Let him take rest. Maybe he is tired, maybe
he is sick or whatever. So we left him at that and then we gave him crocin and one of
them who was attending to him in fact realized that the fever is not subsiding. When they
checked it was around 103 degrees. So then one of them suggested let's get some ice pads
and let's see if we can help him with that. So they got that also. And then it was later
late in the night, about 10.30 in the night and then some of us went back to our rooms
and some of them were there with him. His roommates were there. And then we got a news
around 12.30, 1 in the night that he has died. Imagine 8 o'clock you had dinner with a person,
he was all okay.
Feels feverish, complains of some kind of uneasiness. 9.30, 10 we saw him you know shivering.
Gave him paracetamol, gave him few things and 1 o'clock he leaves his body. So we were
all called. In fact the entire hostel woke up, the word spread and then everyone gathered.
So first time in my life, although I have heard about death, first time in my life it
was a very close encounter with death.
And somebody whom you have
met, you know conversed with just 2 hours, couple of hours before. So then quickly we
called the ambulance and then we rushed him to the hospital. Many of us were not sure
even because it was first encounter with death, whether he is unconscious, he is in coma.
Somebody was saying he is declared dead and somebody is saying that no let's take him
to the hospital. We took him to the hospital and it was confirmed that he is dead, he has died.
So then I was there at 1.30, 2 o'clock in the night and then there was utter commotion
there and then people trying to get hold of his numbers so that parents can be called.
So some of them were calling the parents and we were all there. And since it was 1.30, 2
in the night, so nothing can happen at that time, post-mortem and few other things, whatever
formalities were there, has to happen the next day.
So some of us were to stay back in the hospital. I was there in the hospital. We were supposed
to stay in the room next to the room where this body was kept. It was about 1.32 in the
night. Some people were told to go back to the college, go back to the hostel, go and
sleep and only few of us, 4-5 of us were there in the hospital next morning all the thing
and others were busy doing, calling the parents and few like things. And then I was supposed
to sleep in the room.
to, not sleep, be in the room next to and I was very, very fearful because I had seen
some horror movies and had some idea of ghosts and all those things. I was very, very fearful.
Can anyone tell me what was the cause of fear? Here was a person, four hours back I was dabbling
with him, we have played together, we have conversed together, we have laughed together,
we have stayed together, we have enjoyed each other's company. Why is it that the same person
lying next to me, I am fearful? Why? One thing was, what if in the middle of night he gets up?
You know, the body is lying there. So what is it that? It was a first very close encounter with
death. What is it that this person was there with us? What has happened to him that now we are
fearful of him?
The same person who was so loving, so we used to be, you know, always in his company, now
the same person is lying there and we are fearful. What has happened to that person,
that same person who was so loving, so nice, now there is some element of fear being in
his. Sometimes we say, without you I cannot live. In fact, when a person dies with that
person, with that body, we cannot live. What has happened? We say gujar gya wo. He has left
the body. The body is there. What has left? The soul. And because of the presence of the
soul, that person was alive, was chirpy, was bubbling, was talking, was walking, everything.
And moment the soul leaves the body, the body is lying there with no emotions, with no feelings.
Next day in the morning, we try as far as possible, call all the relatives and cremate.
That same body which has been so royally taken care of is burnt.
If we analyze chemically, all the chemicals which constitute the body are still there.
But we say the person is gone.
So, who is gone is soul, the atma, the real self, which Krishna is talking about.
Now, somebody may say, we all students of science, we have grown up with scientific temperament.
We want evidence, we want proof.
We don't want to believe things blindly.
We don't want to subscribe to things, something which is just as a matter of belief or faith.
So, can someone...
Question, what is the evidence?
Okay, you are talking about soul.
What is the evidence soul exists?
Can you show me soul?
If you show me soul, then I believe.
So, many things in this world we cannot see, but we believe.
For example, if I were to ask you, do you have a mind?
How many of us have a mind?
How many of us have intelligence?
If I were to ask you, can you show me your mind?
I am not talking about grey substance, the brain, the grey matter, that physical brain.
I am not talking about that.
So, mind, Krishna says, is sukshma.
Sukshma means subtle.
Sukshma sharira, manas, buddhi and ahankara, mind, intelligence and false ego.
They are sukshma.
They are subtle.
They are not gross.
Gross body is panchabhuta, earth, air, water, fire and ether.
When a person dies, what we cremate is the sthula sharira, gross body.
So, it is said that mind is subtler than the body.
Suttler than the mind is intelligence.
Even subtler and finer than the intelligence is ego.
And even sukshma, finer than the ego is the soul.
Now, as things become more finer and finer and subtler, the body we can see with our gross eyes.
Yes, that person we are relating with day to day, we are seeing the gross body.
But as it becomes finer and finer, we cannot perceive with our gross senses,
but we can perceive the presence of mind, presence of intelligence, presence of ego,
presence of soul through the symptoms.
How do we know the mind is present?
Sometime we say face is the index of mind.
When a person is frowning, we say why are you so disturbed today?
Why your mind is so disturbed?
Seeing the face, why are you so anxious?
Why are you so anxious?
Or somebody is very quick in grasping things, quickly you tell him something and he is able to grasp,
he is able to give an answer, he says you are very intelligent, brilliant.
So, seeing the symptoms, we can say that intelligence is there, mind is there.
Likewise, the soul can be perceived by its symptom.
What is the symptom of a soul?
Consciousness.
A dead body does not have consciousness.
Because of consciousness, we perceive pleasure and pain.
If there is a pinprick in our finger, we immediately feel pain.
We rush for some kind of a first aid.
Whereas a dead body, there is no experiencer.
You put it on a funeral pyre, light the fire, the body is lying there.
The experiencer, the kshetragana, the knower of the field of activities, the soul has gone.
And because the soul has gone, the symptom of presence of soul is consciousness.
Therefore, there is no consciousness.
So, this soul, Krishna is saying, is
ajo nitya shashvato ayam purano na hanyate hanyamane sharire.
When hanyamane, when the sharira, when the body dies, when a person dies,
na hanyate, it does not die.
What doesn't die? The soul.
Vasamshi jeernani yatha vihaya navani granati naroparani.
Just like we give up old dress and accept new dress.
Suppose my shirt is torn, next day I wear another shirt.
I change this dress and wear another dress.
Likewise, vasamshi jeernani, jeernani means old,
when the body becomes old and it is not fit for the soul to continue its journey,
jeernani yatha vihaya, we give up old garments, accept navani granati,
navani means new, granati means to accept.
Tatha, tatha, exactly like the sharirani vihaya,
sharir means the body, vihaya means to give up.
Navani granati naroparani, a soul accepts another body.
Now let's visualize what is the overall scheme of things.
The soul which is eternal, never dying and this body is like a dress which the soul is wearing.
Because I am wearing a dress which is male, man, born in India, I call myself Indian.
And believe that I am Indian.
Actually that's not my real identity.
Because next life, the same soul accepts another body, let's say in Pakistan and in some other maybe gender
and I call myself that. India is my enemy.
So that is the meaning of ego. Ego means identity.
False ego means false identity.
Dehatma buddhi, this buddhi, this understanding, this identity that I am the body is the fundamental ignorance.
And that's why to break that ignorance, Krishna introduces that you are a spirit soul.
When we are in bodily concept of life, invariably there will be attachment, there will be pain, there will be agony.
And when we come out of this bodily concept of life,
then Krishna says, dhirastatranam huyati, a sober person, a wise person is not bewildered by such a change.
Sometimes we hear of some very near and dear one suffering from some terminal disease, cancer or whatever.
It completely rattles a person.
No doubt, yes, we have attachments. Attachments have a place.
But it completely shatters a person.
Krishna says, dhirastatranam huyati, a sober person, a wise person is not bewildered by such a change.
Any question on this?
How do we conclude what?
So every soul has some distinct number of breaths when it comes into this particular world.
We will cover, this is a little elaborate subject, we will cover in detail.
We were talking about destiny, what is destined, what is not destined.
We will cover that, I think it requires little more detailed explanation.
So what happens is, I understood your question.
So in reincarnation where a person remembers his past life experiences, what happens is, the soul is covered by sukshma sharira.
Sukshma sharira is a concept of manas, buddhi and ahankara.
So this soul with the sukshma sharira goes into another body.
That's why we have born with some abilities, some talents, some inclinations, some phobias, some skills.
Where is it coming from? Some people have artistic skills.
Some people have auditory skills.
I was telling you about child prodigy.
There is a girl.
She is 15 years old, plays some 15-20 instruments, musical instruments.
Can play tabla, can play harmonium, can play violin, can play so many things.
And I can't play even one of them after 25 years.
I don't have that sense.
Sachin Tendulkar joined the Indian team at the age of 16.
Where did he get that skill from?
Some phobias, some fears.
Where did it come from?
So soul with the sukshma sharira accepts another body.
With the sukshma sharira.
That's where our vasanas are there.
That's why imprints are there.
So now let's give the overall picture.
Overall picture is what?
Soul is eternal and life after life.
We have taken many many lives.
Life after life it is accepting a body and then it develops different kinds of attachments,
relationships, friends, brother, father, sister, all this kind of relationship.
And then again dies.
And then again it accepts based on its karma.
Again repeats the same process.
Again dies.
Again accepts another body.
Punarapi janma, punarapi maran.
And sometime as an animal.
Sometime as a plant.
Sometime as a bird.
Sometime as a reptile.
Basis are karma.
Basis are desire.
If my desire is to eat flesh, I will be awarded a body which is conducive for fulfilment of that desire.
And after many many lives, the soul gets an opportunity to get the birth of a human being.
And in human birth,
the soul has the opportunity to get the birth of a human being.
The soul has an opportunity to understand what is all this.
Who am I?
Who is God?
What is the purpose of this creation?
Athato brahma jignasa.
Now that you have got human birth, try to inquire.
If these fundamental questions are not asked and understood,
then we have wasted this human birth.
Which has that ability to inquire and realise the self.
And by following this sadhana bhakti, raganuma bhakti and various other processes,
the soul can extricate itself from the cycle of birth and death and that is called mukti or moksha.
All the solutions what we are looking for in this world are temporary stopgap solutions.
All the advancement we are making, maybe we can prolong.
If a person has heart attack, maybe with latest of medical interventions,
we can prolong life by another 5 years.
But the original problem of repeated birth and death cannot be solved.
In fact, at times, with all the advancement, it has accelerated the process.
I told you, because what if he wakes up in the night?
And it can be unfounded also.
I am not saying it was rational.
Any dead body, if you have close in the vicinity, you will be fearful.
So that made me think, what is it that the same person is lying there,
he was very close friend of mine, why I am fearful of him?
What has happened to him that is arising fear?
It was a question in my mind.
Why is it that same person whom we say, I cannot live without you,
the same person, I cannot live with him now.
I am fearful of living with him, living in the next room, why?
Because soul has left.
It is a dead body.
Yes.
So the question is that in Hindu religion, lot of things are done after the soul has already left the body.
Does it make sense or is it justified?
Like for example, Shraddha ceremony or Pindadana or something like that.
Actually, it makes sense.
It is recommended in our scriptures.
Why? Because the soul, as I said, is eternal.
It is not that the person is dead, he is over, he is gone.
The body is gone.
The person is still there, somewhere.
Enjoying or suffering the resultant reactions of his deeds.
So when a person does some ceremonies, for example, Shraddha ceremony,
where you offer the food to Vishnu and that prasada is offered to that soul,
again, physical barriers, even so called these days, you know, you have pranic healing,
you have Reiki and all those things, you can realize that you can heal a person from a distance.
Yes or no?
So even for that matter, first time when the mobiles came, it was very difficult to believe that,
that connection you can talk to a person.
Earlier phones means there was a wiring.
Then somewhere it came, even trunk calls, so called, there were actual physical connections.
Now imagine you are just walking, how you can talk to a person through this.
So likewise, in a subtler dimension, so earlier the world was very gross.
Gross wires were required for connectivity, but we saw that it's possible
through radio waves or whatever this thing to connect to a person and transmit the voice.
So exactly like this, you can pray for a soul.
